description: Notarize-only record of the dispute's final outcome as the network/issuer rendered it and Stripe relayed it (`charge.dispute.closed`). AGLedger attests the verdict AS RELAYED — it does not claim to be the adjudicator (the issuer/Visa network decided; Stripe reported). Captures won/lost plus the funds movement from balance_transactions so the financial impact is on-chain. | `isLateWin` flags the rare reversal where a lost dispute is later won (Stripe keeps status=won; late_win is a label, not a separate status). | Closes the chain that began at chargeback-dispute-filed-v1 and was authorized through the gated decision: filing -> authorized decision -> network verdict, all hash-linked and offline-verifiable. Reaches terminal RECORDED in one round-trip.

disputeId.description: Stripe Dispute id (du_...; older docs show dp_) that closed.

disputeRef.description: recordId of the chargeback-dispute-filed-v1 this disposition closes.

decisionRef.description: Optional recordId of the evidence-submission-decision-v1 / dispute-concession-decision-v1 that drove this outcome.

finalStatus.description: Terminal Stripe Dispute.status. won = funds returned; lost = funds stay withdrawn; warning_closed = inquiry resolved without becoming a chargeback; prevented = deflected pre-dispute (RDR/Ethoca/CE3).

fundsWithdrawn.description: Amount pulled from balance over the dispute (disputed amount + dispute fee), major units, from balance_transactions.

fundsReinstated.description: Amount returned to balance on a win (amount + fee reversal), major units. 0 on a loss.

closedAt.description: When the dispute reached its terminal status. Optional: the Stripe Dispute object exposes no close timestamp — derive it from the closing `balance_transaction.created` or the `charge.dispute.closed` event time, or use observation time. Omit if unavailable.

isLateWin.description: True when a previously-lost dispute was subsequently won (status reads won; this disambiguates the reversal).
